add-a-product.label: Add a product
add-a-product.summary: Learn how to insert a new product into the inventory_items table, understand the importance of specifying columns, and practice safe data insertion in SQL.

q1.title: Insert a new product row
q1.prompt: Write an SQL statement to add a new product called 'Travel Journal' in the 'Accessories' category, priced at 11.99, with status 'active', is_test 0, no notes, and last_updated '2026-02-11' to the inventory_items table. Use an explicit column list (do not include the id column).
q1.hint: Focus on the SQL task being asked for, not on copying final query text.

help.concept: Build the query from the operation the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which clauses or functions are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the query based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

q2.title: Insert multiple products at once
q2.prompt:
Write an SQL statement to insert two new products into inventory_items at once:
1. 'Desk Lamp', category 'Office', price 22.50, status 'draft', is_test 0, notes 'LED', last_updated '2026-02-13'
2. 'Sample Keychain', category 'Accessories', price 3.00, status 'inactive', is_test 1, notes 'Promo item', last_updated '2026-02-14'
Do not include the id column.
q2.hint: Focus on the SQL task being asked for, not on copying final query text.

help.concept: Build the query from the operation the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which clauses or functions are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the query based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

q3.title: Insert with default and NULL values
q3.prompt: Write an SQL statement to insert a product called 'Eco Notebook' in the 'Office' category, priced at 7.25. Omit the status and is_test columns so they use their default values. Set notes to NULL and last_updated to '2026-02-15'. Do not include the id column.
q3.hint: Focus on the SQL task being asked for, not on copying final query text.

help.concept: Build the query from the operation the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which clauses or functions are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the query based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

q4.title: Why specify columns in INSERT?
q4.prompt: Which of the following are reasons to always specify column names in your INSERT statements? Select all that apply.
q4.hint: Think about what could go wrong if you don't specify columns.

help.concept: Specifying columns in INSERT statements helps prevent errors and makes your code more robust to schema changes.
help.hint_1: Consider what happens if the table structure changes or the order of columns is different.
help.hint_2: Not specifying columns can cause data to be inserted into the wrong fields or lead to errors.

q5.title: Default values in INSERT
q5.prompt: When you omit a column with a default value in your INSERT statement, SQL will use the ______ value for that column.
q5.hint: Think about what SQL does when you don't provide a value for a column that has a the missing term.

help.concept: If a column has a the missing term value and you don't specify it in your INSERT, SQL uses the the missing term.
help.hint_1: The value comes from the table definition, not from your statement.
help.hint_2: It's not NULL unless the the missing term is NULL.

q5.template: When you omit a column with a default value in your INSERT statement, SQL will use the {blank} value for that column.

sk1.title: Why use INSERT statements?
sk1.bodyMarkdown:
The `INSERT` statement in SQL allows you to add new rows to a table. For example, to add a new product to the `inventory_items` table, you can use:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES ('Enamel Pin', 'Accessories', 6.50, 'active', 0, NULL, '2026-02-10');
```

This adds a new product called 'Enamel Pin' to the inventory. Notice that we specify the columns explicitly, which helps prevent mistakes if the table structure changes.

sk2.title: Explicit column lists for safety
sk2.bodyMarkdown:
It's best practice to list the columns you are inserting into. This makes your SQL more robust and readable. For example:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES ('Desk Organizer', 'Office', 18.00, 'draft', 0, 'New office item', '2026-02-12');
```

If you omit the column list, you must provide values for every column in the table, in the correct order. This can lead to errors if the table changes.
